title: Sebastiania commersoniana
taxonomy: Sebastiania commersoniana belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae and is classified as a species within the genus Sebastiania. It is native to South America and is a type of shrub or small tree. The classification of Sebastiania commersoniana is further supported by its morphological characteristics, including its milky latex and stipular spines. The plant's classification also places it within the order Malpighiales, which is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their complex leaves and inflorescences. This classification provides a framework for understanding the evolutionary relationships between Sebastiania commersoniana and other related species.
uses: Sebastiania commersoniana is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
